title: (Bearish Signal) RCON, Moving Average Convergence Divergence Histogram just turned negative
author: tickeron_community
selftext:
This is a Bearish indicator signaling RCON's price could decline from here. Traders may explore shorting the stock or put options. Tickeron's A.I.dvisor identified 48 similar cases where RCON's MACD histogram became negative, and 41 of them led to successful outcomes. Odds of Success: 85%. Current price $6.61 crossed the resistance line at $6.41 and is trading between $9.27 resistance and $6.41 resistance lines. Throughout the month of 03/11/21 - 04/13/21, the price experienced a +123% Uptrend. During the week of 04/06/21 - 04/13/21, the stock enjoyed a +19% Uptrend growth.

https://preview.redd.it/izjcvvknd6t61.png?width=1516&format=png&auto=webp&s=967d78eb37abe9052bd25bb5e849da9a7e2666d7

**Bearish Trend Analysis**

The Momentum Indicator moved below the 0 level on April 12, 2021. You may want to consider selling the stock, shorting the stock, or exploring put options on RCON as a result. Tickeron A.I. detected that in 105 of 120 cases where the Momentum Indicator fell below 0, the stock fell further within the subsequent month. The odds of a continued downward trend are 88%.

The Moving Average Convergence Divergence Histogram (MACD) for RCON turned negative on April 13, 2021. This could be a sign that the stock is set to turn lower in the coming weeks. Traders may want to sell the stock or buy put options. Tickeron's A.I.dvisor looked at 48 similar instances when the indicator turned negative. Tickeron A.I. detected that in 41 of the 48 cases the stock turned lower in the days that followed. This puts the odds of success at 85%.

Following a 3-day decline, the stock is projected to fall further. Considering past instances where RCON declined for three days, the price rose further in 50 of 62 cases within the following month. The odds of a continued downward trend are 82%.

RCON broke above its upper Bollinger Band on March 25, 2021. This could be a sign that the stock is set to drop as the stock moves back below the upper band and toward the middle band. You may want to consider selling the stock or exploring put options.
